description: Cross-registry resolution MUST verify that the upstream registry's `registry_did` (declared in /.well-known/acdp.json) is consistent with the authority used to reach it. Per RFC-ACDP-0006 §3 and §4.1 step 3, the v0.0.1 binding is `did:web:<authority>`. If the capabilities document declares a `registry_did` whose method-specific identifier does NOT equal the authority component of the well-known URL, the resolver MUST treat the resolution as failed.

input.scenario: Resolver fetches https://registry.example.com/.well-known/acdp.json; the document declares a registry_did that does not match the authority

expected.behavior: Resolver MUST verify that the `registry_did` published in the fetched capabilities document binds back to the authority used in the well-known URL. For `did:web`, the method-specific identifier (everything after `did:web:`) MUST equal the authority component, after applying the standard `did:web` rules (colons in the DID-method id correspond to slashes in the path; default port is implied). On mismatch, the resolver MUST NOT trust any subsequent retrieval from this authority and MUST surface `cross_registry_resolution_failed` (RFC-ACDP-0006 §7.1 / §4.1).
expected.rationale: The registry DID is the trust anchor for 'this is the registry I think I'm talking to' (RFC-ACDP-0006 §3.1). Allowing an authority to declare a DID belonging to a different host would let a hijacked DNS record claim the identity of an unrelated registry; consumers pinning `registry_did` would be silently downgraded to talking to the impostor.

additional_failure_cases.case: registry_did is well-formed but uses a method other than did:web
additional_failure_cases.expected_outcome: Resolver MAY accept if it has out-of-band trust for that DID method; otherwise treat as failed. v0.0.1 producers and registries are required to use did:web (RFC-ACDP-0001 §5.4).

additional_failure_cases.case: registry_did is missing entirely from the capabilities document
additional_failure_cases.expected_outcome: The capabilities schema requires registry_did (RFC-ACDP-0007 §3); a missing field is a schema violation and MUST be treated as resolution failed.
